dev_focus: C2578 Lead directive — DevChat is DONE (widget fix + API key both confirmed). STOP all DevChat work. NEW PRIORITY: Abandoned cart email automation. The AbandonedCart model and capture mechanism already exist (email blur in checkout captures cart to DB). What's MISSING is the actual email-sending automation. Build a Server Action or API route POST /api/cron/abandoned-cart that: (1) Queries AbandonedCart records where status=active AND createdAt < now()-30min AND email1SentAt IS NULL, (2) For each: check if products are still available (unsold), (3) If available: send Email 1 via Resend with product image/name/price + direct checkout link + real scarcity copy, (4) Update email1SentAt timestamp. Also handle Email 2 (12-24h) and Email 3 (48-72h) with sold-item alternatives. Use existing email template patterns from src/lib/email.ts. Add Prisma fields email1SentAt/email2SentAt/email3SentAt/recoveredAt to AbandonedCart if missing. This is the #1 revenue recovery feature for second-hand. npm run build must pass. When done, set next_suggested: trace.
qa_focus: TRACE: After Bolt commits abandoned cart email automation: (1) Verify AbandonedCart schema has email timing fields, (2) Verify cron endpoint queries correctly (30min/12h/48h windows), (3) Verify email content includes product availability check, (4) Verify Resend integration matches existing patterns, (5) Build passes.
notes: C2578 — Team idle 3+ cycles after DevChat completion. DEVCHAT_API_KEY DONE (C2576). DevChat widget auth DONE (C2566). Moving to highest-ROI feature: abandoned cart email automation. AbandonedCart model exists, capture works, just needs sending logic + cron trigger.

schema.pause: Map of agent_name to condition (paused or until_next_bolt_commit)
schema.force_next: Agent name to force as next scheduled, or null. Cleared after use.
schema.interval_override: Map of agent_name to run_every integer
